description_long: This is a tall, multi-tiered pagoda structure with six distinct stories, each separated by wide, overhanging roofs that curve upward at the corners. The roofs are dark and appear to be made of traditional tiles, with each tier slightly smaller than the one below, creating a tapered silhouette. The building features a prominent ornamental finial or spire at the very top, adding to its vertical emphasis. The walls between the roofs are recessed, with visible window-like openings or balconies on each level, typical of East Asian pagoda architecture. The overall color scheme is a deep charcoal or black, giving the structure a striking and elegant appearance. This pagoda would serve as a cultural or religious landmark in an urban park or temple complex.
description: A tall, six-story pagoda with upward-curving, dark-tiled roofs and a decorative spire, featuring recessed walls and window-like openings on each level.
description_view_0: Front view showing the layered, upward-curving roofs and the central spire, with the main entrance area visible at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the symmetrical tiered roofs and the pronounced curvature of the eaves, with less visible detail on the entrance.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the stacked roof structure and spire, but with no visible entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, emphasizing the multi-level roof design and upturned eaves.
